contract.input: contiguous F32, FP16, or BF16 [..., 2 * width]
contract.layout: first half gate, second half up branch
contract.output: same prefix shape and dtype with final dimension width
contract.low_precision_rounding: SiLU activation rounded to input dtype before multiply; product rounded to output dtype
contract.aliasing: input and output must not overlap
contract.stream: caller-selected CUDA stream; PyTorch adapter uses the current stream

implementation.mapping: one CUDA block per flattened row
implementation.vectorization: 16-byte packs: four F32 or eight FP16/BF16 values
implementation.fallback: scalar path for odd widths or insufficient alignment

validation.torch_compile: unchecked out operator passed fullgraph compilation
validation.vllm_layer_dispatch: registered SiluAndMul instantiated the Loom replacement and matched vLLM bitwise

raw_cuda.protocol: Cargo bench, CUDA events, CPU oracle before timing; no GEMM clock warmup

vllm_named_baseline.protocol: same preallocated PyTorch C++ custom-operator boundary, one second BF16 GEMM warmup per provider, 100 operator warmups, 15 samples, 2000 launches per sample
vllm_named_baseline.correctness: Loom output was bitwise identical to vLLM in both runs
vllm_named_baseline.interpretation: CUDA Graph replay is effectively parity: the two orderings differ by less than 0.1%. Eager dispatch changed materially with provider order and host scheduling, so it is not used for a speedup claim.

vllm_engine_smoke.runner: normal vLLM V1 Qwen2 model with torch.compile and CUDA Graph capture
vllm_engine_smoke.result: completed generation with the Loom Add+RMSNorm provider and opt-in SiluAndMul replacement loaded
vllm_engine_smoke.interpretation: integration smoke only; one short random-model run is not performance evidence

claim: Loom provides a Rust/CUDA/PyTorch/vLLM-compatible SiLU-and-Mul operator with multi-dtype and engine evidence. It closes compatibility and integration gates but does not outperform vLLM under CUDA Graph replay. The next performance-motivated operator is SiLU-and-Mul fused with output quantization.
